channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine launches unprecedented drone attack on St Petersburg as Putin rejects Zelensky talks
item.description: Russia reported intercepting 376 Ukrainian drones across multiple regions, with strikes killing one person and setting an oil depot ablaze near St Petersburg. Putin ruled out meeting with Zelensky, saying there was "no point" in direct talks.

item.title: 🇰🇵 North Korea announces 10,000-ton destroyer and secret underwater weapons ahead of Xi visit
item.description: State media announced major naval expansion plans including a destroyer and underwater weapons development, just ahead of Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit to Pyongyang.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran demands $24 billion in frozen assets as condition for US peace deal progress
item.description: Iranian military adviser Mohsen Rezaei said negotiations with the US have reached a deadlock, tying progress to the release of $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets. Tensions remain high following US strikes on Iranian coastal sites.

item.title: 🐼🇹🇼 Taipei Zoo Welcomes First Red Pandas From China in Over a Decade
item.description: Taiwan received red pandas from a zoo in Fujian province, marking a rare instance of animal diplomacy amid cross-strait tensions and the first such exchange since 2014.

item.title: 🇧🇭🇰🇼 Iran Fires Missiles at Bahrain and Kuwait, Escalating Regional Conflict
item.description: Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones at Bahrain and Kuwait on Saturday, with Bahrain intercepting the attacks. Kuwait condemned the strikes as a dangerous escalation threatening Gulf stability.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Armenia Holds Election That Could Reshape Ties With Russia and the West
item.description: Armenia votes Sunday in parliamentary elections that could pivot the country away from Russia toward closer Western ties, with Prime Minister Pashinyan seeking a mandate for constitutional reforms.

item.title: ⚔️ US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launch Toward Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces attacked Iranian coastal radar installations on Saturday after intercepting drones launched by Iran toward the strategic Strait of Hormuz, marking the latest escalation in the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇦🤝 Ukraine and Russia Exchange 185 Prisoners Each in Latest POW Swap
item.description: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel on Friday in the latest prisoner of war swap, continuing a pattern of exchanges amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo arrives in Spain for week-long visit focused on migrants and abuse survivors
item.description: Pope Leo arrived in Spain on Saturday for his first apostolic visit to an EU country outside Italy, where he will inaugurate a new tower at Barcelona's Sagrada Familia and meet with migrants who crossed the Atlantic, as well as survivors of clergy sexual abuse.

item.title: 🛢️ US companies to add 40 million barrels to Strategic Petroleum Reserve after Iran war
item.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright announced that companies borrowing oil from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve will add back an extra 40 million barrels as premiums once the Iran conflict ends, addressing concerns about depleted domestic reserves.

item.title: 💰 US oil exports surge, draining Cushing inventories toward historic lows
item.description: US oil exports have reached record levels as refiners worldwide scramble to fill the supply gap from Middle East war disruptions, leaving storage tanks at Cushing, Oklahoma nearly empty and raising concerns about domestic supply security.

item.title: 🏛️ US sanctions network smuggling Iranian LPG to Asia disguised as Omani product
item.description: The US Treasury Department imposed sanctions on a network of individuals, entities, and tankers smuggling Iranian-origin liquid petroleum gas to South and East Asia while falsely labeling it as Omani LPG.

item.title: 🕵️ Pentagon flags Israel as highest counterintelligence risk amid spying concerns
item.description: The US Defense Intelligence Agency raised Israel's threat rating to critical, citing surveillance concerns. Officials report using burner phones and taking extra precautions during visits.

item.title: 🇸🇬 Singapore orders social media platforms to block posts targeting Indian community
item.description: Authorities directed YouTube, Facebook, and X to block 14 foreign posts that targeted the Indian community. Officials said the content undermined social cohesion.

item.title: 🚢 Iran seizes US-linked vessel in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran's Fars News Agency reported the seizure of a vessel belonging to a US-based shipping company in the strategic waterway, escalating maritime tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇪 Ireland scrutinizes Russian-linked alumina plant as sanctions pressure mounts
item.description: The Aughinish Alumina facility in Limerick faces renewed attention over its connections to Russia's military supply chain, as Europe debates sanctions enforcement.

item.title: ⚛️ US lawmakers raise concerns over civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
item.description: Congress members expressed worries about uranium enrichment provisions in the proposed agreement, with experts noting it may not require adherence to nonproliferation standards.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚽ Iran World Cup Players Granted US Visas, Some Staff Still Waiting
item.description: Irans World Cup soccer players received visas to enter the United States days before their first match, though some administrative staff remain without visas.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Reaffirms Support for Hezbollah as Broader Peace Deal Falters
item.description: Iran declared continued backing for Hezbollah and demanded Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon, complicating interim negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇩🇪 Putin Holds Friendly Meeting With Former German Chancellor Schröder
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met one-on-one with Gerhard Schröder in what the Kremlin described as a good and friendly encounter, amid ongoing tensions with the West.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskiy Open Letter to Putin Aimed at Wider Audience Beyond Moscow
item.description: Ukraines president offered face-to-face peace talks in an open letter that analysts say was designed to resonate at the St. Petersburg investment forum and beyond.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Ukraine Drones Target St. Petersburg After Putin Rejects Talks
item.description: Ukrainian drones targeted St. Petersburg in what Russian officials called a "large-scale" attack on the final day of a major Russian investment forum, hours after Putin said he saw no reason to meet Zelenskiy.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan Says Chinese Coast Guard, Research Ships Near Pratas Islands
item.description: Taiwan said a Chinese coast guard ship and a survey ship carried out the first coordinated operation to "provoke" Taiwan in waters around strategically located islands in the South China Sea, signaling increased pressure on the Taiwan-controlled Pratas Islands.

item.title: 🇱🇧🇮🇷 Lebanon President Accuses Iran of Using Country as Bargaining Chip
item.description: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun accused Iran of using Lebanon as a bargaining chip in its negotiations with the United States, in some of his toughest criticism yet of Tehran and its Lebanese ally Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇺🇸⚛️ US Preparing IAEA Resolution Condemning Iran
item.description: The United States is preparing a draft resolution condemning Iran ahead of next week meeting of the UN nuclear watchdog Board of Governors, diplomats said, a step that could complicate wider talks between the US and Iran.
